skill.name: Text-to-Speech — verging.ai
skill.description: Convert text to speech audio using OpenAI TTS-1-HD through the verging.ai proxy API.
skill.summary: Convert text to natural-sounding speech audio using OpenAI TTS-1-HD via the verging.ai proxy API, with 6 voice options and adjustable speed.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate text-to-speech API integration that sends user-provided text to the verging.ai proxy endpoint. All 12 static findings were evaluated as false positives: the flagged 'shell backtick execution' patterns are markdown code fences showing standard bash examples (npx install, env var setup, curl API call), the hardcoded URLs are legitimate service endpoints, and the API key references use clearly-marked placeholders. No malicious intent or obfuscation was found after semantic review.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Examples
medium_findings.description: The README contains bash code blocks showing npx installation, environment variable export, and curl API calls. These are documentation examples in markdown, not executable code in the skill itself. Confidence: 0.92 (false positive - standard API integration documentation pattern).

low_findings.title: API Key Authentication via Environment Variable
low_findings.description: The skill documents using VERGING_API_KEY environment variable with ApiKey Authorization header. The value shown is a placeholder (vrg_sk_your_key_here). This is standard, expected behavior for an API client. Confidence: 0.90 (false positive - legitimate API auth pattern).

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs
low_findings.description: Documentation references verging.ai endpoints including the API base URL and documentation links. These are expected for a TTS proxy service. Confidence: 0.88 (false positive - legitimate service URLs).

content.user_title: Convert Text to Natural Speech Audio
content.value_statement: Developers need a simple way to add voice output to their applications without managing complex audio infrastructure. This skill provides direct access to OpenAI's TTS-1-HD model through a straightforward API call.
actual_capabilities: Convert text strings to MP3 audio files via a single POST request
Select from 6 distinct voice profiles (alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer)
Return CDN-hosted audio URLs for immediate playback or download
Track credit consumption per request for billing transparency
Handle inputs up to 4096 characters per request
limitations: Maximum input length is 4096 characters per request
Requires a verging.ai API key with available credits
Audio is delivered as MP3 format only (no raw PCM or other formats)
Rate limits apply per API key tier

use_cases.title: Voice Narration for Prototypes
use_cases.description: Add spoken feedback to prototypes and demos without recording voice talent or building audio pipelines.

use_cases.title: Automated Audio Content
use_cases.description: Generate podcast intros, video voiceovers, or announcement clips from text scripts in batch workflows.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audio Output
use_cases.description: Convert written content into audio for users with visual impairments or reading difficulties.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Speech Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate speech audio for this text: "Welcome to our platform. We help teams ship faster." Use the nova voice at normal speed.

prompt_templates.title: Voice and Speed Customization
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this product description to speech using the onyx voice at 0.9x speed for a calm, authoritative tone: "Our enterprise solution scales to millions of users."
prompt_templates.scenario: Branded audio with specific voice profile

prompt_templates.title: Long-Form Narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this article excerpt to speech with the fable voice at 1.1x speed: [paste up to 4096 characters of text]

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Segment Audio Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three speech clips: intro with shimmer at 1.0x, body with alloy at 1.0x, and outro with echo at 1.0x. Return all three audio URLs.

output_examples.input: Text: "Good morning! Today we'll explore the fundamentals of machine learning." Voice: nova, Speed: 1.0
output_examples.output: Audio URL: https://cdn.verging.ai/proxy/tts/abc123.mp3 | Credits consumed: 1 | Duration: ~4 seconds

output_examples.input: Text: "Thank you for calling. Your call is important to us." Voice: onyx, Speed: 0.9
output_examples.output: Audio URL: https://cdn.verging.ai/proxy/tts/xyz789.mp3 | Credits consumed: 1 | Duration: ~5 seconds

best_practices: Store your API key in an environment variable, never in source code or committed files
Check the credits_consumed field in the response to track usage and avoid unexpected depletion
Keep individual requests under 4096 characters; split longer text into multiple calls if needed
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API keys in scripts, notebooks, or version-controlled files
Sending sensitive personal data through the TTS endpoint without reviewing the provider's data policy
Ignoring rate limit responses (HTTP 429) and retrying immediately without backoff

faq.question: What audio format is returned?
faq.answer: The API returns MP3 files delivered via CDN URLs. The audio_url field in the response provides a direct download link.

faq.question: How many credits does one request use?
faq.answer: The rate is 1 credit per 1000 characters of input text, with a minimum of 1 credit per request.

faq.question: Can I use voices other than the six listed?
faq.answer: No. The six available voices are alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, and shimmer. Custom voice cloning is not supported.

faq.question: What happens if my text exceeds 4096 characters?
faq.answer: The API returns a 400 error. Split your text into smaller segments and make multiple requests, then concatenate the audio files.

faq.question: Is there a free tier?
faq.answer: This depends on verging.ai's pricing model. Check their website for current plans and credit allocations.

faq.question: Can I adjust pronunciation or add pauses?
faq.answer: No. The API does not support SSML or pronunciation overrides. Use punctuation and line breaks to influence pacing.
